description: Workspace context identification rules for autonomous agents. Ensures correct company/client mapping and prevents cross-contamination between contexts.

contextModel.explanation: Each workspace represents a consultancy engagement: the COMPANY is where the owner works, the CLIENT is the end-customer receiving the service. Agents must never confuse these layers.

ws-socnew.policy: DO NOT OPERATE without explicit written authorization from lucassfreiree

ws-corp-1.policy: DO NOT OPERATE without explicit written authorization from lucassfreiree

identificationProtocol.description: Step-by-step protocol agents MUST follow to identify workspace context
steps: 1. Scan user message for company/client keywords
2. Match keywords against workspace identificationKeywords arrays
3. If match found → set workspace context
5. If ambiguous (keywords from multiple workspaces) → ASK user
6. If no keywords found → ASK user which workspace
7. Once identified → load workspace config from state/workspaces/<ws_id>/workspace.json
8. Apply workspace-specific rules (token, repos, stack, data classification)
identificationProtocol.neverAssume: No workspace is default. Even ws-default requires explicit identification.

corporateEnvironmentRules.description: CRITICAL: Rules for all content that touches corporate environments (commits, PRs, descriptions, issues, documentation)
rules: NEVER mention AI, artificial intelligence, Claude, GPT, LLM, or any AI tool in corporate-facing content
NEVER include AI references in: commit messages, PR titles, PR descriptions, issue titles, branch names, code comments pushed to corporate repos
Commits to corporate repos (bbvinet/*, CIT repos) must appear as regular developer work
Autopilot internal references (CLAUDE.md, session memory, agent contracts) are OK — they stay in the autopilot repo
This rule applies to ALL workspaces (ws-default, ws-cit) equally

bad: feat: Claude AI auto-fix for lint errors
good: feat: fix lint errors in auth module

crossContaminationRules: NEVER reference Banco do Brasil data/repos/infra in CIT context
NEVER reference Itau data/repos/infra in Getronics context
NEVER use BBVINET_TOKEN for CIT operations
NEVER use CIT_TOKEN for Getronics operations
NEVER create patches that contain identifiers from the wrong workspace
NEVER mix CI/CD pipeline status between workspaces
NEVER share session state between workspaces without explicit cross-reference
NEVER mention AI/Claude/GPT/LLM in any corporate-facing content (commits, PRs, issues)
Dashboard MUST show workspace indicator on every page
Commits MUST include workspace_id in commit message when workspace-specific
Issues MUST be labeled with workspace_id

dashboardRules.contextIndicator: Dashboard header MUST show active workspace with company + client name
dashboardRules.workCenterFiltering: Tasks, alerts, and pipeline data MUST be filtered by workspace
